headline.label: 9 out of 10 typical workloads should stay on the API

api_models.name: Claude Haiku 4
api_models.typical_price_source: Anthropic and OpenRouter published API pricing
api_models.quality_caveat: These open-weight options can be evaluated for support, summarization, and coding-adjacent workflows, but they are not quality-equivalent to Claude.

api_models.name: Claude Sonnet 5
api_models.typical_price_source: Anthropic and OpenRouter published API pricing
api_models.quality_caveat: Frontier hosted models differ materially in reasoning, tool use, safety behavior, and latency; use these mappings only as candidate self-host investigations.

api_models.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash
api_models.typical_price_source: Google AI Studio and OpenRouter published API pricing
api_models.quality_caveat: Flash-class API models optimize latency and price; mapped open weights may need task-specific evaluation before replacement.

api_models.name: GPT-5 mini
api_models.typical_price_source: OpenAI and OpenRouter published API pricing
api_models.quality_caveat: These models are plausible efficient substitutes for narrow or cost-sensitive workloads, not quality-identical replacements for GPT-5 mini.

api_models.name: GPT-5
api_models.typical_price_source: OpenAI and OpenRouter published API pricing
api_models.quality_caveat: The mapped open-weight models are not asserted to match GPT-5 quality; they are credible candidates when cost, compliance, or capacity forces a self-host investigation.

api_models.name: Other / not listed
api_models.quality_caveat: Because this hosted model is not mapped precisely, RunWhere cannot make a confident quality or cost-boundary claim.

serving_modality_details.label: Hosted API
serving_modality_details.description: Hosted model API via provider marketplace or direct vendor.
serving_modality_details.best_fit: Default for most workloads, especially bursty or uncertain demand.

serving_modality_details.label: Managed dedicated endpoint
serving_modality_details.description: Dedicated inference endpoint on Vertex AI, SageMaker, Azure ML, Bedrock custom model class, or similar.
serving_modality_details.pricing_model: Provisioned accelerator instance-hours plus managed-service premium and reduced ops labor.
serving_modality_details.best_fit: Teams needing isolation, private networking, or deployment controls without raw VM operations.

serving_modality_details.label: Serverless GPU
serving_modality_details.description: GPU-backed container or inference worker that can scale to zero.
serving_modality_details.pricing_model: Active GPU seconds plus request/cold-start overhead and lightweight ops labor.
serving_modality_details.best_fit: Bursty small or medium open-weight workloads that want API-like operations.

serving_modality_details.label: Cheap always-on GPU VM
serving_modality_details.description: Single smaller GPU VM such as L4, A10G, T4, or L40S.
serving_modality_details.pricing_model: Hourly GPU VM rental times 720 monthly hours plus operational labor.
serving_modality_details.best_fit: Steady small-model production where idle capacity is low.

serving_modality_details.label: Serious always-on GPU VM
serving_modality_details.description: A100/H100-class VM or node for frontier-size models or high throughput.
serving_modality_details.pricing_model: Hourly accelerator node rental times 720 monthly hours plus operational labor.
serving_modality_details.best_fit: Steady frontier-model or high-throughput production workloads.

serving_modality_details.label: Scheduled/batch GPU
serving_modality_details.description: Scheduled GPU job for offline inference, backfills, nightly summarization, evaluations, or other latency-relaxed work.
serving_modality_details.pricing_model: Active job GPU-hours plus orchestration overhead and batch ops labor.
serving_modality_details.best_fit: Predictable offline inference where requests can queue and capacity can be scheduled.

serving_modality_details.label: Owned/on-prem hardware
serving_modality_details.description: On-prem, colo, or owned workstation/server GPUs.
serving_modality_details.pricing_model: Capex amortization plus power, cooling or colo, maintenance, and labor.
serving_modality_details.best_fit: Privacy-bound, air-gapped, or very steady high-volume workloads.

exception_rules.rationale: Data residency can make provider controls, private networking, or owned/on-prem deployment more important than token price.
Air-gapped workloads are not a pure API-vs-GPU cost comparison.
Sub-100ms p99 latency can force regional dedicated serving even when hosted API token cost is lower.
A steady small-model substitute can fit on cheap single-GPU capacity before API spend is very high.
At sustained four-figure API spend, a right-sized always-on GPU VM is worth checking.
Medium open-weight substitutes can be viable when utilization is predictable.
Batch jobs can avoid always-on idle capacity and API burst pricing.
Predictable medium-model batches are one of the cleaner self-host exceptions.
Frontier-scale self-hosting needs serious capacity, but very high steady spend can justify a deeper read.
Five-figure monthly API spend is high enough to test dedicated capacity, even if API may still win.
